title: Hattie the Mouse and the Rainbow Mushroom Mystery
summary: After finishing a book about forest animals, a new adventure begins with Hattie the Mouse, who discovers her workshop is out of essential red clay mushrooms for making hats. Accompanied by her friends Mischief Mouse and Mistress Mouse, she sets off in a tow truck toward the grocery store. Their journey takes an unexpected turn when they encounter Goldbug, a friend stranded with a broken engine and a missing wheel. After a successful roadside rescue, the group reaches a grocery store overflowing with mushrooms in every color imaginable. Hattie gathers her supplies and returns to her hollow tree workshop, mission accomplished.

highlightQuote.text: I would like to take the tow truck because first there's a car stuck, Mischief Mouse, Lily Mouse, straight.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right. And I'll turn out the main lights. And then maybe we'll leave the lamp on for now if we're going to read that book so I can see the words.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad. Why are you reading?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right, let's try it. Forest Friends. Oh yes. I think we're running out of batteries on our little special magic light here.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, there's more batteries up in your office. I told you that.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That's true, there are more batteries up there, but I don't think we can replace the battery on this special little magic light. Well, if you look at it, it doesn't have a way to open it, and you need to open it up to replace the batteries. You know, if the battery really runs down, we could just cut it open and see if we could fix it by doing some special surgery on it, but not right now.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, can you... can you read?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Little Bear plays hide and seek. He calls out, three, two, one! He starts looking all around. Where could his friends have gone? Use your magic finding device to help Little Bear find his friends. Shine it on the pages and see if you can reveal their hiding places.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, look this boo-boo. Dad, this is healing, this boo-boo.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it's a little... a little scab from maybe a scratch. It's healing, yeah.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Look. Dad, this boo-boo leave this mark. And this boo-boo leave this mark, and this, and this.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Just tiny scratches, yeah.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And this little sting.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, sometimes they sting, but then they feel better. Let's see, there's the baby herons. Now, can you find the baby ducklings? There they are.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Where?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: There they are.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Where?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Right there.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, I can't see. Dad, can you see?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, Dad, I came home and look for them.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, let's look for them. There's the baby ducks, and down here, oh, the frogs.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, look for that squirrel! That squirrel's up in the tree.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I see squirrel. What else is here? Oh, there's another froggy. Oh, who's that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, I think that's... that's... they're little flowers.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, I want to turn the page. Turn the page, the page, the page, the page. Dad, first let's do... Dad, first let's do that one.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Who's in there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Owl.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Owl. Whoosh, little bird. Little bird, raccoon, dragonfly, birdy, oh, and look.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Spider! And birdy, birdy, birdy, birdy. Look, there are the bees in that hive.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, let's find them. Oh, there's a bee, there's a bee. There's some more bees, buzz buzz buzz buzz.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Why does that bear hear some bees?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He hears some buzzing, yes, buzz buzz buzz.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And does he want their honey?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I think he might like their honey, but he can't get it because the beehive is up too high. And then who are we going to find?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, first find these birds.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, a red bird and a yellow bird. Hi birds.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's Robin and Tulu.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Robin and Tulu, you're right. Oh, there's a bird here. And... Mama Bear. The end.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And is that Mama Wolf?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That's Mama Wolf, yes. Ow-ooo!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, now let's turn out the light so we can do Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you're ready for Stars and Stories? Okay, we still have enough time to do a quick Stars and Stories time. All right. Do you want to tell a Stars and Stories story about a mystery?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No... Hattie!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Always about Hattie. How about Hattie the Mouse and the big mystery? Once upon a time, Hattie the Mouse heard a knock at her door because someone was coming to tell her there was a mystery. Who was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was Mischief!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mischief Mouse came into Hattie's workshop, all out of breath. He said, Hattie Mouse, there's a mystery! Can you help us solve it? And what did Hattie Mouse say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Um, um, Mischief Mouse says, ah, let me catch my breath. There's a mystery because we lost our shaker egg. Do you think you can help us find it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. Just use your clues and you'll find it. Use my cup clues and my Lego clues.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, your cup clues and your Lego clues. What do they tell you, Mischief Mouse?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They tell you to look in the... the sunroom first, said Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Look in the sunroom first, said Hattie Mouse. All right, and so they left Hattie Mouse's workshop in her little hollow tree and they walked down the path into what house is the sunroom in?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's in Mistress's house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Mistress's house has a sunroom. So they knocked on Mistress Mouse's door. Hattie Mouse said, oh, there's a doorbell, we could ding it. Do you want to make the doorbell sound?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ding ding dong.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ding, ding dong! And then Mistress Mouse came to the door and said, oh yes, how can I help you everyone? And what did Hattie Mouse say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We have a mystery in our workshop.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A mystery, said Mistress Mouse. What's the mystery?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The mystery is that I don't have any more red clay mushrooms for making hats.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you're out of red clay mushrooms? Well, that's too bad. Where do you think you could get some more, said Mistress Mouse?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Maybe there's some at that grocery store, and there's orange ones there too, and there's blue ones and purple ones and green ones and red ones and indigo ones and violet ones and gray ones and even teal ones and even white ones and even black ones and even... orange ones.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: At the grocery store, you say.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And even yellow ones.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow. Perhaps we should all go together to the grocery store to get these mushrooms that you're talking about. Shall we go? We can all jump in my car, said Mistress Mouse. Or would you like to take the tow truck?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I would like to take the tow truck because first there's a car stuck, Mischief Mouse, Lily Mouse, straight.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, there's a car stuck, you say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, and it's Goldbug's car.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it's Goldbug's car. Well, perhaps we can help him out while we're on our way to the grocery store, said Mistress Mouse. Okay everyone, jump in! So who got in the car?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Everyone.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Everyone got in the car, and they shut the door, and then they put on their seatbelts, click. And Mistress Mouse said, okay everybody ready? Let's go! Vroom! She started it up.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then they stopped to see the repair for Goldbug's car.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Um, and Goldbug hopped out of his car and Mistress Mouse said, oh hi Goldbug, I heard you were in trouble. What seems to be the problem? And what did Goldbug say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: My car is broken.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It's broken. Uh oh, that's the timer.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You can do your story when the light is on.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, we just have to finish up our story pretty soon. And so Goldbug said, yeah, um, one of my wheels fell off, I think. Well, let me count...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And your engine broke.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And my engine broke. Yeah, there's one, two, three wheels. I'm supposed to have four, I think. And my engine broke. Can you help me out Mistress Mouse? And Mistress Mouse said, okay everyone, let's quickly find Goldbug's wheel and I'll fix his engine. And then everyone ran around up and down the street looking around for the wheel.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, and then they found the wheel and then they put it on Goldbug's car and then Mistress... no, and then Mistress Mouse kept working on Goldbug's engine.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That's right. And then she said, oh, I think I've got it Goldbug, why don't you try starting it up? And Goldbug hopped in the car and he turned the key, vroom! And it started right up. And Goldbug said, hey thanks!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I think I'm all set.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay everybody, see you later! And he drove off, jing-jing-jing-jing.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then they got back in their car and put back those seatbelts on and then they drove some more.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They drove some more and then, ta-da, we're at the grocery store. Okay, now it's time to...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And they also has more red ones too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And they had some red mushrooms at the grocery store.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And they also had the different mushrooms.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They had all sorts of colors. And Hattie the Mouse said, gosh, I don't need all of these, let's pick three different colors of mushrooms. What...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: How about ten?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ten? Well, what colors should we get?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Orange, yellow, tan, and white, and black, and this is like a tan, and interracial, and so many more.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Those are all colors of skin too, that's very interesting, isn't it? And then...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Those were all the mushrooms that you need.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Aha.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's even further than eleven.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It is. And so they brought them... the mushrooms over to the cashier who went boop boop boop and checked them out and put them all in a bag. And then said, thank you! And then they got back in the car and then they drove home. And Hattie the Mouse said, thanks everyone, now I've got all the mushrooms I need. She went back to her workshop and put them away. And that is the end of the story.

characters.name: Little Bear
characters.description: A character in the first book who plays hide and seek with his friends.

characters.name: Robin
characters.description: A bird friend mentioned in the Forest Friends book.

characters.name: Tulu
characters.description: Another bird friend mentioned by Izzy during the book reading.

characters.name: Mama Bear
characters.description: The colossal mother bear discovered at the first story’s conclusion, a well-meaning giant whose accidental grazing once laid waste to the murgirls' vibrant kelp forest.

characters.name: Mama Wolf
characters.description: This silver-furred matriarch, a figure of striking grace from the story’s illustrations, bears the heavy mantle of a mother searching for her lost cub. Her amber gaze is clouded with a fierce, maternal ache as she tends to the rhythmic task of pumping milk, desperate to keep her life-giving strength ready for the moment her little one is returned to her side.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Mischief Mouse
characters.description: Reigning from a purple-striped Jovian castle, this affable mouse King—a playful-named companion to Pinecone Mouse—is a seasoned adventurer, guardian of the Underground Library, and Hattie’s steadfast ally. Between savoring artisanal cheeses and awaiting a rugged cardboard replacement for his fragile Lego hat, he spends leisure hours meticulously constructing and gleefully smashing soaring towers. A pancake-loving monarch who once tended shop for mountain-dwelling traffic cones, his cosmic quests recently shifted from the stars to the speedway; having borrowed a tow truck from Mistress Mouse, he proved a formidable competitor who nearly clinched a stunning victory. Whether piloting starships or joining the group’s latest trek to Nana’s house, he remains a vigilant protector and devoted friend across the galaxy.

characters.name: Mistress Mouse
characters.description: A polite three-year-old mouse and resourceful shopkeeper, this community cornerstone balances a bold artistic spirit with sharp mechanical grit. Whether crafting intricate Lego creations in her sunroom or piloting her signature pink tow truck to solve puzzles and assist neighbors, she is a master of the road and a multi-talented creator. From famously adorning cars with blue stripes to dominating the race track, her inventive soul is always on the move. Usually seen in a mushroom-adorned cardboard hat with an orange sparkle purse, this resourceful driver remains an essential, evocative fixture of every neighborhood adventure.

characters.name: Goldbug
characters.description: A friend whose car breaks down on the side of the road with an engine problem and a missing wheel.

characters.name: Cashier
characters.description: The person at the grocery store who scans the rainbow-colored mushrooms.

places.name: The Forest
places.description: Cloaking the surface of the green planet in a vast mantle of dense greenery, this whispering woodland serves as the lush setting of the 'Forest Friends' chronicle. It is a realm where creatures play eternal hide-and-seek between Hattie’s cozy clinic and the ancestral cradle of Finn’s companions. Along magical trails where Hattie and the Pakruder Worm harvest hat-making ingredients, winding paths veil secret gopher tunnels and hidden mole cottages, forming the essential route toward the wizard's castle. While its sun-drenched threshold remains a sanctuary for the Rainbow Giraffe and Mama Bird, the deeper, darker thickets harbor a somber mystery; it was within these sprawling, uncharted depths—the same eerie expanse where the sick creature was first spotted and Mossy was discovered—that Mole recently vanished into the shadows.

places.name: Hattie's Workshop
places.description: Tucked within the cozy hollow of a weathered tree, Hattie Mouse’s dwelling serves as a busy, imaginative atelier and social hub where the air hums with the rhythmic snip of scissors. Here, amidst a cheerful clutter of measuring tapes, wet pigments, and scavenged shells, she crafts her signature starry hats from an eclectic mix of fur and feathers. The workshop is bathed in the earthy radiance of her fungal store—a brilliant collection of red, orange, and yellow mushrooms—illuminating the haunting indigo witch painting where Hattie first encountered the squeaking worm. It is in this storied corner, beneath the witch's gaze, that she recently unearthed a mysterious message hidden alongside a cache of sparkly green clover stickers.

places.name: Mistress's House
places.description: A cozy residence where Mistress Mouse and her mother, Mischief, share celebratory feasts of golden corn, featuring a light-drenched sunroom where the mouse friends gather to scour for hidden clues.

places.name: The Sunroom
places.description: A specific bright room in Mistress Mouse's house.

places.name: The Grocery Store
places.description: A magical store stocked with mushrooms in every color of the rainbow, including indigo, teal, and interracial skin tones.
